detection_signals: Sauternes is made from grapes at a specific sugar level
If the grapes don't reach the target Brix, it can't be Sauternes
All Sauternes vintages use the same harvest criteria
why_incorrect: While harvest maturity is critical, the focus is on noble rot development, not a fixed Brix reading. A rigid Brix threshold ignores the biological development and vintage variability.
misconception: Sauternes grapes must be harvested when they reach a specific sugar level (e.g., 20 Brix), and earlier or later harvests produce inferior wine.
corrected_understanding: Sauternes harvest timing prioritizes noble rot development and grape maturity, not a fixed Brix level.
